channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes have released an open letter calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts.
item.dc:creator: Scott Mcfetridge, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea launches missiles toward eastern waters in 2nd launch of year, South says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday test-fired multiple missiles toward its eastern waters, South Korea’s military said, as it continued its weapons demonstrations ahead of Donald Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Utah's push to wrest control of public land from the federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday turned back a push by the state of Utah to wrest control of vast areas of public land from the federal government, marking a small victory for land conservation advocates who worry that similar efforts may escalate in a Republican-controlled Washington.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Traffic into Manhattan drops 7.5% in first week of new toll. That's 43,000 fewer cars a day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A new toll on drivers entering the core of Manhattan brought modest but measurable traffic reductions to New York City’s heavily-gridlocked streets in its first week of operation, according to preliminary data released Monday by the state's transit authority.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires tearing through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures in what might be the most expensive conflagrations in the nation's history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Patient groups call on Trump administration to let Medicare cover obesity drugs
item.description: “The new administration must take action to address this crisis, by allowing Medicare and Medicaid to offer comprehensive obesity care for the millions of Americans who need these services and treatments,” the groups said.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Richmond recovers from water crisis, residents celebrate clean water
item.description: "We were spending eight hours cleaning all of the water," Nesrala said. "Eight hours just running the water, it was like milky and it was nasty.
item.dc:creator: Sara Molina, NewsNation

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mike McCarthy won't return as Dallas Cowboys coach, AP source says
item.description: Mike McCarthy is not returning as coach of the Dallas Cowboys, who are going on three decades since their last Super Bowl title, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: The recall specifically concerns 20-ounce packages of Casa Mamita Chicken & Cheese Taquitos produced by Bestway Sandwiches, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: The recall specifically concerns 20-ounce packages of Casa Mamita Chicken & Cheese Taquitos produced by Bestway Sandwiches, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's when Trump nominees will appear for confirmation hearings
item.description: More than a dozen people nominated by President-elect Trump to serve in his administration will make their case to Senate committees this week. The busy week will kick off with the Senate Armed Services Committee hearing from Pete Hegseth on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Millions have had student loans canceled under Biden
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite failing to deliver his promise for broad student loan forgiveness, President Joe Biden has now overseen the cancellation of student loans for more than 5 million Americans — more than any other president in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Illegal immigrant arrested for kidnapping, attacking teen near Trump's backyard: Police
item.description: Santo Martinez Varela of Honduras faces kidnapping and sexual assault charges after allegedly attacking a 13-year-old at a Florida park earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More firefighters rush to Los Angeles ahead of stronger winds
item.description: A growing force of firefighters and equipment moved into the Los Angeles area as another round of powerful winds threatened to trigger new wildfires
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal immigrant with $1M worth of drugs, guns given free housing courtesy of blue state taxpayers: officials
item.description: An illegal immigrant from the Dominican Republic was allegedly caught with $1 million worth of fentanyl and an AR-15 rifle in Massachusetts' emergency housing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeff Bezos' space company calls off debut launch of massive new rocket in final minutes of countdown
item.description: Blue Origin called off the debut launch of its massive new rocket early Monday because of technical trouble.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump team is questioning civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: Incoming senior Trump administration officials have begun questioning career civil servants who work on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for in the 2024 election, their political contributions and whether they have made social media posts that could be considered incrim
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Sam Moore, who sang ‘Soul Man’ in Sam & Dave duo, dies at 89 due to surgery complications
item.description: Sam Moore, the surviving half and higher voice of the 1960s duo Sam & Dave that was known for such definitive hits of the era as “Soul Man” and “Hold On, I'm Comin,’” has died. He was 89.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Texas, Arizona arming Mexican drug cartels
item.description: The U.S. and Mexico are working closer than ever to trace the origin of guns used in drug cartel massacres and other crimes south of the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Navy veteran cleared of conspiracy charges in high-profile Jan. 6 case is sentenced to time served
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired Navy intelligence officer who was cleared of the most serious charges in his U.S. Capitol attack trial alongside Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes avoided additional time behind bars when he was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rare gold coin found in red kettle donations for The Salvation Army
item.description: A rare South African Krugerrand gold coin worth thousands was found amid the spare change donated to a Salvation Army red kettle in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, Fox 5

item.title: Delta Air Lines says 4 passengers injured after flight was aborted at Atlanta airport
item.description: A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into southern U.S. states Friday, prompting governors to declare states of emergency and shuttering schools across the region.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfires latest: 153,000 residents still under evacuation as Kenneth Fire order lifted
item.description: Firefighters are hoping for a break Friday from the fierce winds that have fueled massive blazes in the Los Angeles area, killing 10 people, obliterating whole neighborhoods and setting the nation’s second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Miami Dolphins player arrested after crash in South Carolina
item.description: Former NFL player Robert Quinn was arrested in South Carolina following an early Friday morning crash.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors seek 15 years in prison for former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez after bribery conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors say former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez should be imprisoned for 15 years for a “grave abuse of his power,” after the New Jersey Democrat became the first person to be convicted of abusing a Senate committee leadership position and the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Smoke, smoldering homes and flames as wildfires ravage Los Angeles area
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
